data.title: El Salvador Redistributes Bitcoin Holdings to Mitigate Risks
data.description: According to PANews, El Salvador has redistributed its Bitcoin holdings, previously stored in a single address containing 6,274 Bitcoins valued at $678 million, into 14 new addresses. This move, reported by Cointelegraph, aims to minimize the potential impact of quantum attacks by dividing the funds into smaller amounts. The Bitcoin Office of El Salvador stated in a post on X that each Bitcoin address will now hold a maximum of 500 BTC, enhancing security measures.

data.title: Web3 Hardware Wallet Evaluation Concludes with Comprehensive Report
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, the "Wallet Wars" hardware wallet evaluation event, organized by Web3 security infrastructure provider GoPlus, concluded this week. The event featured evaluations of hardware wallets from Coolwallet, imKey, KeyPal, Keystone, OneKey, and SafePal. Over three weeks, an expert panel assessed the wallets across six dimensions, culminating in the release of the "Web3 Hardware Wallet Security Evaluation Report." This report aims to offer users a clearer and more objective reference for selecting wallets. The event reached millions of users, marking a significant step in promoting Web3 security education and industry transparency.

GoPlus, the initiator of the evaluation, remains focused on security. Its API currently supports over 40 public blockchains, with more than 30 million daily calls, protecting over 28 million wallets and preventing potential losses exceeding $5 billion. Looking ahead, GoPlus plans to enhance Web3 security through education, tools, and partnerships, collaborating with more partners to build a secure and reliable Web3 ecosystem.

data.title: Hong Kong Monetary Authority Promotes Blockchain for Payment Solutions
data.description:
According to Foresight News, the Vice President of the Hong Kong Monetary Authority, Chan Wai-man, highlighted efforts to encourage mainland enterprises to establish overseas business headquarters and corporate treasury centers in Hong Kong. Speaking at the 7th Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area Financial Development Forum, Chan emphasized the coordination of overseas funds. Chan addressed the current challenges in global payment systems, noting the numerous intermediaries and high costs involved. He expressed optimism that technological advancements, such as blockchain, will gradually resolve these issues
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/28997641780450?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Hong Kong Monetary Authority Promotes Blockchain for Payment Solutions
data.description:
According to Foresight News, the Vice President of the Hong Kong Monetary Authority, Chan Wai-man, highlighted efforts to encourage mainland enterprises to establish overseas business headquarters and corporate treasury centers in Hong Kong. Speaking at the 7th Guangdong-Hong Kong-Macao Greater Bay Area Financial Development Forum, Chan emphasized the coordination of overseas funds.

Chan addressed the current challenges in global payment systems, noting the numerous intermediaries and high costs involved. He expressed optimism that technological advancements, such as blockchain, will gradually resolve these issues. Hong Kong is actively advancing the application of central bank digital currencies and commercial bank currencies, aiming to provide more convenient and efficient payment solutions for future cross-border trade and investment.

data.title: Hong Kong Elderly Man Defrauded in Cryptocurrency Exchange Scam
data.description:
According to Foresight News, a 77-year-old man in Hong Kong was defrauded of approximately HKD 2.92 million during a cryptocurrency transaction on August 25. The incident occurred at a virtual currency exchange shop on Jervois Street in Sheung Wan.

The elderly man brought HKD 3 million in cash to purchase stablecoins. Two male employees placed the cash in a specially designed safe and then left the shop under false pretenses. They subsequently opened the back of the safe outside the shop and stole the cash. To delay the victim, they transferred about HKD 78,000 worth of stablecoins to the woman's electronic wallet.

Realizing he had been deceived after waiting for the employees to return, the man reported the incident to the police. Authorities reviewed CCTV footage, including images from the Sharp Eyes program, and conducted a thorough investigation and intelligence analysis.

Within three days of the incident, police arrested three suspects, including both men and women, and recovered part of the stolen funds.

data.title: Verb Technology to Rebrand as TON Strategy Company
data.description: According to BlockBeats, Nasdaq-listed company Verb Technology will change its name to TON Strategy Company on September 2. Concurrently, the company's Nasdaq stock ticker will be updated from VERB to TONX.

data.title: Former Cred Executives Sentenced for Wire Fraud Conspiracy
data.description:
According to PANews, U.S. District Court Judge William Alsup has sentenced former Cred LLC CEO Daniel Schatt and CFO Joseph Podulka to federal prison terms of 52 months and 36 months, respectively, for conspiracy to commit wire fraud. In addition to imprisonment, Judge Alsup ordered both Schatt and Podulka to serve three years of supervised release and pay fines of $25,000 each. The defendants are scheduled to begin their sentences on October 28, 2025, with a restitution hearing set for October 7, 2025.

Under their plea agreements, Schatt and Podulka admitted to conspiring to provide incomplete and misleadingly positive descriptions of Cred's business operations, failing to disclose significant negative information about the challenges and risks faced by Cred. These omissions were detrimental to Cred's customers. Cred filed for bankruptcy on November 7, 2020, with over 6,000 claims from customers and investors totaling more than $140 million. According to the government's sentencing memorandum, the total claims exceeded $1 billion when calculated using the August 2025 valuation of the various cryptocurrencies involved.
